articles.title: Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 21 killed in Kyiv region
articles.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

articles.title: New Java-Based QuimaRAT MaaS Built to Run on Windows, Linux, and macOS
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a novel Java-based remote access trojan (RAT) called QuimaRAT that's capable of targeting Windows, Linux, and macOS environments. According to LevelBlue, the cross-platform malware is advertised under a malware-as-a-service (MaaS) model, costing anywhere between $150 for one month to $1,200 for lifetime access. Other subscription tiers include $300 for

articles.title: New TrojPix Attack Leaks Data From Air-Gapped Systems via Video Cable Emissions
articles.summary: Researchers at Shandong University have shown a fast new way to pull data off computers that are cut off from every network. The technique, called TrojPix, tweaks on-screen pixels in ways the eye cannot see, so that the video cable carrying them radiates a faint radio signal a nearby receiver can decode. But TrojPix works only once malware is already on the target machine, so it

articles.title: SkillCloak Lets Malicious AI Agent Skills Evade Static Scanners with Self-Extracting Packing
articles.summary: Scanners meant to catch malicious add-on "skills" for AI coding agents can be fooled by a few simple changes that leave the malware working, according to a new study from researchers at the Hong Kong University of Science and Technology. Their strongest trick slipped past every scanner tested more than 90% of the time, and the same team built a runtime checker that catches most of the

articles.title: From AI to ‘killer robots’: UN chief issues urgent governance call
articles.summary: UN chief António Guterres appealed on Monday for far-reaching, worldwide controls on Artificial Intelligence, as increasingly powerful AI chips that are designed for civilian use shift to the battlefield, where “killer robots” are already the norm.

articles.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
articles.summary: A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces were ordinary. Home devices became a routing cover. Clean code pulled dirt from a dependency. Identity shortcuts aged badly. AI systems trusted the wrong instructions. Same soft spot throughout: trust

articles.title: Suspected China-Nexus Hackers Use Fake Indian Tax Filing Utility to Deploy DcRAT
articles.summary: A suspected China-nexus threat activity cluster has been observed targeting Indian taxpayers, tax professionals, and corporate finance teams to deliver a remote access trojan designed to steal sensitive data from compromised hosts. The multi-stage campaign, codenamed Operation DragonReturn by Seqrite Labs, involves sending spear-phishing emails impersonating the Income Tax Department of India.

articles.title: Opera GX Flaw Let Malicious Sites Auto-Install Mods to Steal Data From Visited Pages
articles.summary: Researchers found a flaw in Opera GX, the gaming-focused version of the Opera browser, that let a malicious website silently install a browser add-on and use it to lift specific data from the pages a victim visits. In a proof of concept, they reconstructed a signed-in user's full Gmail address from a single visit, with no click. Opera has patched the flaw and says it found no evidence that

articles.title: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban
articles.summary: United States President Donald Trump says Fifa "made the right decision" to suspend Folarin Balogun's one-match ban, adding that otherwise, it would have left a "big stain" on the World Cup.

articles.title: Thousands of forgotten WW1 soldiers recognised for first time
articles.summary: Nearly 10,000 soldiers' names are being added to the official database of war dead, a cherished step for many of their descendants.

articles.title: Bangladesh courts China even as ties with India improve
articles.summary: Relations between Dhaka and Delhi, that turned frosty under the interim government, are on the mend.

articles.title: As deadly as war and as powerful as governments, global organized crime remains in the shadows
articles.summary: When people think of the world’s deadliest threats, armed conflicts usually come to mind first. Yet every year, organized crime quietly claims a comparable number of lives.

articles.title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
articles.summary: Using satellite data, researchers connected harmful algal blooms with warm water and low water levels at one of Colorado’s largest reservoirs.

articles.title: Unpatched Flaws Disclosed in Filesystem Bundled Into Millions of Embedded Devices
articles.summary: Security firm runZero has disclosed seven vulnerabilities in FatFs, a small filesystem library that lets a device read and write the FAT and exFAT formats used on USB drives and SD cards. The flaws matter because FatFs is nearly everywhere. It ships inside the firmware that runs security cameras, drones, industrial controllers, hardware crypto wallets, and other devices built on

articles.title: New Avalon Malware Framework Packs CrownX Ransomware Capabilities
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously undocumented modular malware framework codenamed Avalon that's distributed by means of a multi-stage phishing chain capable of bypassing traditional security controls. Avalon combines credential collection, lateral movement, remote access, recovery disruption, and ransomware execution, bringing together diverse functions under one

articles.title: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
articles.summary: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

articles.title: Super Typhoon Bavi makes landfall on US Pacific islands with huge wind gusts
articles.summary: The storm, with winds of nearly 290km/h (180mph) and gusts of 350km/h, is lashing the island of Rota.

articles.title: Australia space agency has found 'likely source' of mystery space balls
articles.summary: The agency says that they "appear to be pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle".

articles.title: 'Hotter and hotter and hotter' - Europe's new climate in seven charts
articles.summary: Temperature records were smashed in June - and scientists warn this is a sign of things to come.

articles.title: Global push for AI governance amid warnings of ‘catastrophic harm’
articles.summary: Can artificial intelligence benefit all of humanity – safely, fairly and without causing “catastrophic harm”? That is the question at the heart of a major UN summit opening in Geneva on Monday.

articles.title: Sudan’s people subject to ‘relentless’ drone strikes in El Obeid
articles.summary: The war in Sudan threatens to spiral further as the strategically-important town of El Obeid continues to face “relentless” drone attacks by advancing paramilitary militias, the UN’s human rights chief, Volker Türk, said on Friday.

articles.title: NASA Webb Uncovers Unusual Galaxy Shaped by Cosmic Collision
articles.summary: In new images from NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope to celebrate its fourth science anniversary, a familiar galaxy transforms into something far richer, and far more complex, than ever seen before. Webb’s unprecedented sensitivity across near- and mid-infrared wavelengths cuts through the thick lanes of dust that obscure Centaurus A’s center in visible light, showing […]

articles.title: The Download: South Korea’s hottest bachelors, and advancing eye transplants
articles.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in a matchmaking company a year ago. In a…

articles.title: U.S. Government Entity Paid Kairos $1 Million in Data-Theft Extortion Case
articles.summary: A U.S. government entity paid about $1 million to keep stolen files from being leaked, according to a new case study by Rakesh Krishnan for Ransom-ISAC, built on a leaked negotiation chat and the blockchain trail the payment left. The odd part: the group that took the money calls itself Kairos, but it may not be a ransomware gang at all. Krishnan found no sign that it ever locked a single

articles.title: A supercharged El Niño is coming – are we ready?
articles.summary: We need to urgently build resilience to more extreme weather and rising seas, protecting nature as an ally in adapting to climate impacts The post A supercharged El Niño is coming – are we ready? appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives
articles.summary: The impacts of extreme heat vary widely across countries and cities, and approaches tailored using data can target solutions at vulnerable groups The post With extreme heat now a public health crisis, local data can save lives appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion
articles.summary: At this month’s Our Ocean Conference in Mombasa, campaigners warned that ocean protection requires governments to halt new offshore fossil fuel projects The post Ocean summit stays silent on new wave of offshore oil and gas expansion appeared first on Climate Home News .

articles.title: Huge crowds fill Tehran streets for Khamenei's funeral procession
articles.summary: Many people were waving Iranian flags and red banners symbolising vengeance for the former supreme leader's war death.

articles.title: Wildfire in southern France forces evacuation of 10,000 people
articles.summary: Tour de France organisers ban spectators from stage three as a wildfire hits the Pyrénées-Orientales region.

articles.title: Australian PM apologises for 'inappropriate' comments about Kylie Minogue
articles.summary: Albanese has apologised 'unequivocally' after his remarks prompted a backlash.

articles.title: New heatwave likely to peak at 34C with week-long health alert issued
articles.summary: Another heatwave is on its way and set to last significantly longer as Stav Danaos explains.

articles.title: A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda's Ebola frontline
articles.summary: As Uganda works to contain an Ebola outbreak that is also affecting neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers are doing more than responding to a deadly virus. They are helping families navigate fear, misinformation and stigma.

articles.title: Invisible peacebuilders: How ‘diplomacy works’ to prevent conflict
articles.summary: Described as “sometimes modest, sometimes historic,” UN special political missions have quietly been preventing the escalation of conflicts during the organization’s 80-year history, becoming a key instrument for maintaining peace.

articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Emergency meeting on Iranian attack in Bahrain
articles.summary: The 15-member Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Thursday morning to address the escalating tensions in the Gulf region following Iran attacks in recent days on Bahrain and Kuwait while Tehran and Washington continue to trade strikes over the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint. Stay tuned for live updates.

articles.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers
articles.summary: Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in Sunoo, a matchmaking company based in Seoul, a year ago. In a move typical of anxious South Korean parents, his mother signed him up, hoping to find a good wife for her son. Lately, says Baek (who asked to be…

articles.title: How to Evaluate an AI SOC Platform in 2026: 6 Capabilities That Separate Leaders from Bolt-On AI solutions
articles.summary: Building a shortlist for an AI SOC evaluation can be tough. SIEM, SOAR, and pureplay AI SOC vendors are all saying the same thing. But behind the identical label sit very different products, from chat assistants bolted onto a legacy SIEM to agent platforms that run detection, triage, investigation, and response on their own data foundation. Whether a platform will materially change outcomes for

articles.title: New "Bad Epoll" Linux Kernel Flaw Lets Unprivileged Users Gain Root, Hits Android
articles.summary: A newly disclosed Linux kernel flaw called Bad Epoll (CVE-2026-46242) lets an ordinary user with no special access take full control of a machine as root. It affects Linux desktops, servers, and Android, and a fix is out. Bad Epoll sits in the same small stretch of kernel code where Anthropic's most powerful AI model, Mythos, recently found a different bug. The AI caught one flaw and missed

articles.title: 'I ate ketchup and cheese,' says Venezuelan girl trapped under quake rubble for 32 hours
articles.summary: Fabiana was trapped in the rubble of a 10-storey residential building after two earthquakes rocked Venezuela in June.

articles.title: Sri Lanka prison riots leave 26 dead and more than 100 injured
articles.summary: The two days of violence at Negombo Prison are the worst prison riots in the country in years.

articles.title: Turbines turning from wind to sustainable products
articles.summary: Thousands of tonnes of non-recyclable wind turbine blades are coming to the end of their lives.

articles.title: Record-breaking temps in US as 4th July holiday begins
articles.summary: Record-breaking heatwave hits US as Fourth of July holiday weekend gets underway.

articles.title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
articles.summary: Blue and white stars shine brightly against crimson gas in this image from NASA’s Hubble Space Telescope.

articles.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
articles.summary: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly transplanted eye wasn’t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a device that maintains…

articles.title: North Korean Hackers Publish 108 Malicious Packages and Extensions in PolinRider Campaign
articles.summary: The North Korean threat actors linked to the Contagious Interview campaign have been observed publishing 108 unique packages and web browser extensions spanning npm, Packagist, Go, and Google Chrome as part of an ongoing activity referred to as PolinRider. "The campaign remains active, and new malicious packages are likely to continue appearing as threat actors compromise maintainer accounts,

articles.title: As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments
articles.summary: People support efforts to protect forests and curb industrial food production, the results of the latest Global Citizens' Assembly show - but politicians have been slow to act The post As food shocks spread, citizens are showing more leadership than governments appeared first on Climate Home News .
